key_insight: A hypernetwork-conditioned hybrid physics-data surrogate can learn how mycelium composite microstructure changes multiscale mechanical response, enabling efficient optimization across graded materials.
engineering_relevance: This makes functionally graded, sustainable composite structures practical to design by reducing the cost of full multiscale simulations while still targeting macro-level stress reduction.
method: Condition a hybrid surrogate on microstructural variables using a hypernetwork.
Use the surrogate inside multiscale optimization for graded mycelium-woodchip composites.
Extend conditioning to manufacturing variables to link process settings to final behavior.
evidence_signal: The conditioned surrogate was validated against full FE^2 simulation and the optimized graded disk reduced peak stress by 42% versus a random-microstructure design.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the representativeness of the small training set, so extrapolation to unseen microstructures or manufacturing conditions should be treated cautiously.
paper_title: Microstructure-Conditioned Surrogate Models for Graded Multiscale Optimization of Mycelium Composites
